channel.title: Sitemap on mingzaialo's blog
channel.description: Recent content in Sitemap on mingzaialo's blog

item.title: Introduction to NLP and Deep Learning
item.description: Word Embedding Basic Idea If each attribute is a dimension, then we might give each word a vector, like this: \[ q_\text{mathematician} = \left[ \overbrace{2.3}^\text{can run}, \overbrace{9.4}^\text{likes coffee}, \overbrace{-5.5}^\text{majored in Physics}, \dots \right] \] \[ q_\text{physicist} = \left[ \overbrace{2.5}^\text{can run}, \overbrace{9.1}^\text{likes coffee}, \overbrace{6.4}^\text{majored in Physics}, \dots \right] \] Basic Iead Then we can get a measure of similarity between these words by doing: \[ \text{Similarity}(\text{physicist}, \text{mathematician}) = q_\text{physicist} \cdot q_\text{mathematician} \]

item.title: Semi-supervised learning with ladder networks
Generative Adversarial Parallelization
item.description: Table of Contents GANs GANs GANs GANs GANs Paralletizing Generative Adversarial Networks(GAP) Paralletizing Generative Adversarial Networks Paralletizing Generative Adversarial Networks Paralletizing Generative Adversarial Networks Paralletizing Generative Adversarial Networks Paralletizing Generative Adversarial Networks Experiments Experiments Experiments Experiments Experiments Experiments Experiments Experiments Reference GANs GANs min-max \begin{equation} \label{eq:1} \min_{G}\max_{D}\mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{x}\sim P_{data}(\mathbf{x})}[\log D(\mathbf{x})]+\mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{z}\sim P_z(\mathbf{z})}[\log (1-D(G(\mathbf{z})))] \end{equation} GANs Empirical Observations The reality of training GANs is quite different from the ideal case due to the

item.title: InfoGAN: Interpretable Representation Learning by Information Maximizing Generative Adversarial Nets
item.description: Table of Contents GAN GAN Mutual Information for Inducing Latent Codes Mutual Information for Inducing Latent Codes Mutual Information for Inducing Latent Codes Variational Mutual Information Maximization Variational Mutual Information Maximization Variational Mutual Information Maximization Variational Mutual Information Maximization GAN GAN Min-Max \begin{equation} \label{eq:1} \min_G\max_D \mathbb{\mathbf{x}\sim P_{data}(\mathbf{x})}[\log D(\mathbf{x})]+\mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{z}\sim P_z(\mathbf{z})}[\log (1-D(z))] \end{equation} Mutual Information for Inducing Latent Codes Mutual Information for Inducing Latent Codes Input : 2 parts

item.title: CNN
CNN
Unsupervised Representation Learning with Deep Convolutional Generative Adversarial Networks
Visualizing and Understanding Recurrent Networks

item.title: Adversarial Autoencoders
item.description: Adversarial Autoencoders Adversarial Autoencoders Adversarial Autoencoders Notations \(\mathbf{x}\) : input code vector of an autoencoder \(\mathbf{z}\) : latent code vector of an autoencoder \(p(\mathbf{z})\) : the prior distribution we want to impose on the codes \(q(\mathbf{z}|\mathbf{x})\) : an encoding distribution \(p(\mathbf{x}|\mathbf{z})\) : the decoding distribution \(p_d(\mathbf{x})\) : the data distribution \(p(\mathbf{x})\) : the model distribution Adversarial Autoencoders aggregated posterior The encoding function of the autoencoder \(q(\mathbf{z}|\mathbf{x})\) defines an aggregated posterior distribution of \(q(\mathbf{z})\) on the hidden code vector of the autoencoder as follows :

item.title: Adversarial Images for Variational Autoencoders
item.description: Autoencoders and Variational Autoencoders Autoencoders and Variational Autoencoders Autoencoders and Variational Autoencoders Variants of Autoencoders sparse autoencoders(with \(l_{1}\) -norm) denoising autoencoders(use implicit regularization by feeding noise to the input) Variational Autoencoders pass

item.title: Adversarial Multiclass Classification: A Risk Minimization Perspective
item.description: Multiclass SVM generalizations Multiclass SVM generalizations multiclass support vector machine (SVM) seeks class-based potentials \(f_y(\mathbf{x}_i)\) for each input vector \(\mathbf{x}\in \mathcal{X}\) and class \(y\in \mathcal{Y}\) so that the discriminant function \(\hat{y}_f(\mathbf{x}_i)=\arg\max_{y} f_y(\mathbf{x}_i)\) minimize the misclassification errors, \(loss_f(\mathbf{x}_i,y_i)=I(y_i\neq \hat{y}_f(\mathbf{x}_i))\) Multiclass SVM generalizations Empirical Risk Minimization \begin{equation} \label{eq:1} \min_f\mathbb{E}_{(\mathbf{x},y)\in P_{data}(\mathbf{x},y)}[loss_f(x,y)] \end{equation} for Zero-one loss is NP-hard hinge loss approximation In the binary setting, \(y_i\in {-1,+1}\) where the potential of one class can be set to zero(\(f_{-1}=0\)) with no loss in generality, the hinge loss is defined as \([1-y_if_{+1}(x_i)]_+\).

item.title: Adversarial Neural Machine Translation
item.description: Adversarial Neural Machine Translation Adversarial Neural Machine Translation Adversarial Neural Machine Translation Ref : ground-truth translation Hyp : model translation sentence NMT Model Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) based encoder-decoder as the NMT model to seek a target language translation \(y^{‘}\) given the source sentence \(x\),which means \(y^{‘}\sim G(y|x)\). To be specific, given source sentence \(x\) and previously generated words \(y_{<t}\) the probability of generating word \(y_t\) is :

item.title: Adversarially Learned Inference
item.description: Adversarially learned inference Adversarially learned inference(ALI) Two distributions Encoder joint distribution \(q(\mathbf{x},\mathbf{z})=q(\mathbf{x})q(\mathbf{z}|\mathbf{x})\) Decoder joint distribution \(p(\mathbf{x},\mathbf{z})=p(\mathbf{z})p(\mathbf{x}|\mathbf{z})\) Notes Encoder margin : \(q(\mathbf{x})\) is the empirical distribution; Decider margin : \(p(\mathbf{z})\) is the sampler distribution(Maybe Gaussian); Adversarially learned inference(ALI) Objective Matching two joint distributions. Matching game Joint pairs \((\mathbf{x},\mathbf{z})\) are drawn either from \(q(\mathbf{x},\mathbf{z})\) or \(p(\mathbf{x},\mathbf{z})\); A discriminator network learns to discriminate between the two; The encoder and decoder networks are trained to fool the discriminator.

item.title: An Architecture for Deep, Hierarchical Generative Models
Attention Is All You Need
Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes
Autoencoding beyond pixels using a learned similarity metric
item.description: Overview Overview Variational autoencoder \(\mathbf{z}\sim Enc(\mathbf{x})=q(\mathbf{z}|\mathbf{x})\) \(\hat{\mathbf{}x}\sim Dec(\mathbf{z})=p(\mathbf{x}|\mathbf{z})\) then : \begin{eqnarray*} \mathcal{L}_{VAE}&=&-\mathbb{E}_{q(\mathbf{z}|\mathbf{x})}[\log p(\mathbf{x}|\mathbf{z})]+D_{KL}(q(\mathbf{z}|\mathbf{x})||p(\mathbf{z}))\\ &=&\mathcal{L}_{like}^{pixel}+\mathcal{L}_{prior} \end{eqnarray*} GAN maxi- mize/minimize the binary cross entropy: \begin{eqnarray*} \mathcal{L}_{GAN} & =& \log (Dis(\mathbf{x}))+\log (1-Dis(Gen(\mathbf{z})))\\ \end{eqnarray*} with respect to Dis / Gen with \(\mathbf{x}\) being a training sample and \(\mathbf{z}\sim p(\mathbf{z})\) Beyond element-wise reconstruction error Beyond element-wise reconstruction error Keys Replacing the VAE reconstruction (expected log likelihood) error term with a reconstruction error expressed in the GAN discriminator

item.title: Batch Normalization
C-RNN-GAN: Continuous recurrent neural networks with adversarial training
Cmake
item.description: 初识cmake 初识cmake 背景知识 官网: www.cmake.org 特点 开放源代码。 跨平台, 并可生成 native 编译配置文件, 在 Linux/Unix 平台, 生成 makefile, 在苹果平台, 可以生成 xcode, 在 Windows 平台,

item.title: Code : Learning a Deep Compact Representation for Visial Tracking
item.description: Table of Contents MatLab Code Set the parameters MatLab Code Set the parameters Load data Name Type Means dataPath String the dir of all the tracking benchmark datasets title String which dataset we use fullPath=[dataPath,title,'/img/']; d=dir(fullPath,"*.jpg"); if size(d,1)==0 d=dir(fullPath,"*.png"); end if size(d,1)==0 d=dir(fullPath,"*.bmp"); end seq.opt=opt; seq.init_rect=[p(1)-p(3)/2,p(2)-p(4)/2,p(3),p(4),p(5)]; for i=1:size(d,1): seq.s_frames{i}=[fullPath,d(i).name]; end run_DLT

item.title: DRAW : A Reccurrent Neural Network for Image Generation
item.description: Table of Contents Introduction Introduction Introduction Introduction The DRAW Network The DRAW Network Network Architecture Network Architecture Network Architecture Network Architecture Network Architecture Network Architecture Network Architecture Network Architecture Read and Write Operations Read and Write Operations Reading and Writing Without Attention Selective Attention Model Selective Attention Model Selective Attention Model Reading and Writing With Attention Next article Next article Reference

item.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Table of Contents What is Reinforcement Learning? What is Reinforcement Learning? Terminology & notation Terminology & notation Terminology & notation What is Reinforcement Learning? What is Reinforcement Learning? Branch of machine learning concerned with taking sequences of actions Usually described in terms of target interacting with a previously unknown enviroment, trying to maximize cumulative reward Formalized as partially observable Markov decision process (POMDP) Terminology & notation Terminology & notation Terminology & notation

item.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-based Image Captioning with Embedding Reward
item.description: Table of Contents [](#) TODO

item.title: Deep Tracking : Seeing Beyond Seeing Using Recurrent Neural Networks
item.description: Table of Contents Deep Tracking The Bayes Model The Bayes Model The Bayes Model Filtering Using a Recurrent Neural Network Training Supervised mode Unsupervised mode(difficult) Deep Tracking The Bayes Model Graphical Model The Bayes Model Joint Probability \begin{equation} P(y_{1:N},x_{1:N},h_{1:N})=\prod_{t=1}^NP(x_t|y_t)P(y_t|h_t)P(h_t|h_{t-1}) \end{equation} \(P(h_t|h_{t-1})\) : the hidden state transition probability capturing the dynamics of the world; \(P(y_t|h_t)\) : modelling the instantaneous unoccluded sensor space; \(P(x_t|y_t)\) : describes the actual sensing process.

item.title: Deep Visual-Semantic Alignments for Generating Image Descriptions
Directed GMs: Bayesian Networks
item.description: Notation Variable, value and index Random variable Random vector Random matrix Parameters Knowledge Engineering Picking variables Observed Hidden Picking structure CAUSAL Generative Coupling Picking Probabilities Zero probabilities Orders of magnitudes Relative values Hidden Markov Model Probability of a parse Joint PDF \begin{equation} \label{eq:1} p(\mathbf{x},\mathbf{y})=p(y_1,\cdots,y_T)p(x_1,\cdots,x_T|y_1,\cdots,y_T) \end{equation} Marginal probability \begin{equation} \label{eq:2} p(\mathbf{x})=\sum_yp(\mathbf{x},\mathbf{y})=\sum_{y_1}\sum_{y_2}\cdots\sum_{y_N}\pi_{y_1}\prod_{t=2}^Ta_{y_{t-1},y_t}\prod_{t=1}^Tp(x_t|y_t) \end{equation}

item.title: Energy-Based Generative Adversarial Networks
item.description: Table of Contents Theory Theory Theory Theory Theory Theory Theory Theory Theory Theory Repelling Regularizer Repelling Regularizer Theory Theory The Loss \begin{eqnarray} \label{eq:2} \mathcal{L}_D(\mathbf{x},\mathbf{z}) & = & D(\mathbf{x})+[m-D(G(\mathbf{z}))]^+\\ \mathcal{L}_G(\mathbf{z})&=&D(G(\mathbf{z})) \end{eqnarray} The Target \begin{eqnarray} \label{eq:3} V(G,D) & = & \mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{x}\sim P_{data}(\mathbf{x})}[D(\mathbf{x})]+\mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{z}\sim P_z(\mathbf{z})}[ [m-D(G(z))]^+ ]\\ U(G,D)& = & \mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{z}\sim P_z(\mathbf{z})}[D(G(\mathbf{z}))] \end{eqnarray} Theory Nash equilibrium A Nash equilibrium of the system is a pair \((G^{*},D^{*})\) that satisfies:

item.title: First Step Toward Model-free, Anonymous Object Tracking with Recurrent Neural Networks

item.title: GAN and VAE from an Optimal Transport Point of View
item.description:
Minimum Kantorovitch Estimators MKE  empirical distribution \(
u:=  rac{1}{n}\sum_{j=1}^n\delta_{y_j}\), \(y_j\in \mathcal{X}\subset \mathbb{R}^p\) parametric distribution family \((\mu_{	heta})_{	heta\in \Theta}\subset \mathcal{P}(\mathcal{X})\), \(\Theta\subset \mathbb{R}^q\)  MKE for \(	heta\) is defined as any solution of the problem
 egin{equation} \label{eq:1} \min_{	heta}W_c(\mu_{	heta},
u) \end{equation}
 \(W_c\) : Wassertein distance for some cost function \(c\):   egin{equation} \label{eq:2} W_c(\mu,
u)=\min_{\gamma\in P(\mathcal{X}	imes \mathcal{X})}{\int_{\mathcal{X}	imes \mathcal{X}}c(x,y)d\gamma(x,y); P_{1#\gamma}=\mu,P_{2#\gamma}=
u} \end{equation}
 \(P_{1#}\) , \(P_{2#}\) : marginalization operators  MKE-GM  \(\mu_{	heta}\) is defined by a map and a given distribution \(\zeta\) : \(g_{	heta}: \mathcal{Z}ightarrow \mathcal{X}\), i.

item.title: GloVe: Global Vectors for Word Representation
item.description: Table of Contents Related work Matrix Factorization Methods Related work Matrix Factorization Methods Keys These methods utilize low-rank approximations to decompose large matrices that capture statistical information about a corpus.1. The particular type of information captured by such matrices varies by application. [return]

item.title: Hierarchical Variational Models
item.description: Table of Contents BBVI BBVI Technical summary Technical summary Technical summary Technical summary Hierarchical Variational Models Hierarchical Variational Models Hierarchical Variational Models Specifying an HVM Specifying an HVM BBVI BBVI Definition It sets up a parameterized family of distributions over the latent variables and then optimizes the parameters to be close to the posterior. Technical summary Technical summary Consider a posterior distribution \(p(\mathbf{z}|\mathbf{x})\), a distribution on \(d\) latent variables \(z_1,\cdots,z_d\) conditional on a set of observations \(\mathbf{x}\).

item.title: Improved Techniques for Training GANs
item.description: Table of Contents Tricks Feature Matching Minibatch discrimination Minibatch discrimination Minibatch discrimination Historical averaging One-sided label smoothing BN Reference Tricks Feature Matching Notations \(f(\mathbf{x})\) : activations on an intermediate layer of the discriminator; New objective for the generator \begin{equation} \label{eq:1} ||\mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{x}\sim P_{data}(\mathbf{x})}[f(\mathbf{x})]-\mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{z}\sim P_z(\mathbf{z})}[f(G(\mathbf{z}))]||^{2} \end{equation} As with regular GAN training, the objective has a fixed point where \(G\) exactly matches the distribution of training data.

item.title: Improving Generative Adversarial Networks with Denoising Feature Matching
Introduction
item.description: Table of Contents Introduction Elements of Reinforcement Learning Example : Tic-Tac-Toe Introduction Elements of Reinforcement Learning policy reward signal value function optional model(of the environment) Example : Tic-Tac-Toe

item.title: Knowing When to Look: Adaptive Attention via A Visual Sentinel for Image Captioning
Layer Normalization
item.description: Table of Contents Layer Normalization Layer Normalization Layer Normalization Layer Normalization Compute the layer normalization statistics \begin{equation*} \label{eq:1} \begin{array}[tb]{rcl} \mu^l&=&\frac{1}{H}\sum_{i=1}^Ha_i^l\\ \sigma^l&=&\sqrt{\frac{1}{H}\sum_{i=1}^H(a_i^l-\mu_l)^2}\\ \end{array} \end{equation*} H : the number of hidden units in a layer.

item.title: Learning to Generate with Memory
item.description: Table of Contents Probabilistic DGMs with Memory Overall Architecture Overall Architecture Concrete Examples with Hierarchical Memory Mechanisms Concrete Examples with Hierarchical Memory Mechanisms Probabilistic DGMs with Memory Overall Architecture training data : \(\mathcal{D}\) each sample \(\mathbf{x}\in \mathcal{D}\) is independently generated with latent factors \(\mathbf{z}_{L},\cdots,\mathbf{z}_1\): Draw the top-layer factors \(\mathbf{z}_L\sim \mathcal{N}(0,1)\) For \(l=L-1,\cdots,0\), calculate the mean parameters \(\mathbf{\mu}_l=g_l(\mathbf{z}_{l+1};M_l)\) and draw the factors \(\mathbf{z}_l\sim P_l(\mathbf{\mu}_l)\) \(g_l\) is a feed-forward deep neural network with \(I_l\) deterministic layers and a set of memories \({M_l^{(i)}}_{i=0}^{I_l-1}\).

item.title: Learning to Refine Object Segments
item.description: Table of Contents Network Structure Network Structure Network Structure DeepMask DeepMask Structure DeepMask notations DeepMask Segmentation Branch DeepMask Loss DeepMask Implementation Details Fully convolutional networks for semantic segmentation PASS SharpMask Refinement Overview Refinement Details Refinement Details Refinement Details Training and Inference Feedforward Architecture Experiments Details Network Structure Network Structure Network Structure Feedforward nets, such as DeepMask0 with skip architectures,such as Fully convolutional networks for semantic segmentation0 This model (c,d) DeepMask DeepMask Structure DeepMask notations Each sample \(k\) in the training set is a triplet containing

item.title: LightRNN: Memory and Computation-Efficient Recurrent Neural Networks
item.description: Table of Contents LightRNN RNN Model with 2-Component Shared Embedding RNN Model with 2-Component Shared Embedding RNN Model with 2-Component Shared Embedding RNN Model with 2-Component Shared Embedding LightRNN RNN Model with 2-Component Shared Embedding RNN Model with 2-Component Shared Embedding Then for a vocabulary with \(|V|\) words, we only need \(2\sqrt{|V|}\) unique vectors for input word embeddings, it is the same case for the output word embeddings.

item.title: Maxout Networks
MobileNets: Efficient Convolutional Neural Networks for Mobile Vision Applications
item.description: Table of Contents Architecture Depthwise Separable Convolution Depthwise Separable Convolution Depthwise Separable Convolution depthwise convolutions Pointwise convolution Cost Analysis Architecture Depthwise Separable Convolution Depthwise Separable Convolution Keys factorize a standard convolution into a depthwise convolution and a \(1\times 1\) convolution called a pointwise convolution. Depthwise Separable Convolution Original Input size : \(D_F\times D_F\times M\) output : \(D_F\times D_F\times N\) Kernel Size: \(D_K\) \begin{equation} \label{eq:1} G_{k,l,n}=\sum_{i,j,m} K_{i,j,m,n}\cdot F_{k+i-1,l+j-1,m} \end{equation}

item.title: Predicting Deeper into the Future of Semantic Segmentation
item.description: Table of Contents What is this paper about? What is this paper about? Predicting future frames and segmentations Predicting future frames and segmentations What is this paper about? What is this paper about? Predicting the Semantic Segmentation of the future image. Predicting future frames and segmentations Predicting future frames and segmentations Notations \(X_i\) : the i-th frame of a video sequence \(X_{t:T}\) : the sequences of frames from \(X_t\) to \(X_T\) \(S_i\) : the log-probabilities of frame \(X_t\) Experiements

item.title: RATM: Recurrent Attentive Tracking Model
item.description: RECURRENT NEURAL NETWORKS IRNN Structure Basic RNN Initialization of \(W_rec\) A scaled version of the identity matrix Hidden activation function ReLU Initialization of \(h_0\) Zero vector NEURAL ATTENTION MECHANISMS Attention Mechanism DRAW Links : A MODULAR FRAMEWORK FOR VISION Feature-extraction module Aims The feature-extraction module computes a feature representation of a given input glimpse. Pre-train is useful After pre-training, the feature extractor’s parameters can either be continued to be updated during end-to-end training, or kept fixed.

item.title: SCA-CNN: Spatial and Channel-wise Attention in Convolutional Networks for Image Captioning
Self-Normalizing Neural Networks
item.description: Table of Contents Self-normalizing Neural Networks Definition Constructing Self-Normalizing Neural Networks Constructing Self-Normalizing Neural Networks Constructing Self-Normalizing Neural Networks Deriving the Mean and Variance Mapping Function Deriving the Mean and Variance Mapping Function Stable and Attracting Fixed Point (0, 1) for Normalized Weights Self-normalizing Neural Networks Definition A neural network is self-normalizing if it possesses a mapping \(g : \Omega \rightarrow \Omega\) for each activation \(y\) that maps mean and variance from one layer to the next and has a stable and attracting fixed point depending on \((\omega,\tau)\) in \(\Omega\).

item.title: Show and Tell: A Neural Image Caption Generator
Show, Attend and Tell: Neural Image Caption Generation with Visual Attention
item.description: Table of Contents Model Model Details Encoder Decoder Attention Attention Initialization of LSTM Learning Stochastic “Hard” vs Deterministic “Soft” Attention Stochastic “Hard” Attention Model Model Details Encoder Encoder extract \(L\) vectors Extract features from a lower convolutional layer unlike previous work which instead used a fully connected layer 1 Decoder LSTM Attention What difference between “show and tell” is “Attention”

item.title: ShuffleNet: An Extremely Efficient Convolutional Neural Network for Mobile Devices
item.description: Table of Contents Approach Channel Shuffle for Group Convolutions Structure ShuffleNet Unit Structure Network Architecture Approach Channel Shuffle for Group Convolutions Structure ShuffleNet Unit Structure Network Architecture

item.title: Skeleton Key: Image Captioning by Skeleton-Attribute Decomposition
Towards Principled Methods for Training Generative Adversarial Networks
item.description: Table of Contents The GAN The GAN Sources of Instability Sources of Instability The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem The Perfect Discrimination Theorem [](#)

item.title: Transferring Rich Feature Hierarchies for Robust Visual Tracking
item.description: Introduction Introduction Goal Given this single (labeled) instance, the goal is to track the movement of the object in an online manner. The Contributions To alleviate the overfitting and drifting problems during online tracking, we pre-train the CNN to distinguish objects from non-objects instead of simply reconstructing the input or performing categorical classification on large-scale datasets with object-level annotations. Introduction The Contributions The output of the CNN is a pixel-wise map to indicate the probability that each pixel in the input image belongs to the bounding box of an object.

item.title: Tutorial: Deep Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Reinforcement Learning in a nutshell Reinforcement Learning in a nutshell RL is a general-purpose framework for decision-making RL is for an agent with the capacity to act Each action influences the agent’s future state Success is measured by a scalar reward signal Goal: select actions to maximise future reward Reinforcement Learning in a nutshell DL is a general-purpose framework for representation learning Given an objective Learn representation that is required to achieve objective Directly from raw inputs Using minimal domain knowledge Deep Reinforcemnet Learning: AI = RL + DL Deep Reinforcement Learning: AI = RL + DL We seek a single agent which can solve any human-level task RL defines the objective DL gives the mechanism RL + DL = general intelligence Introduction to Deep Learning Deep Representations A deep representation is a composition of many functions Deep Representations Its gradient can be backpropagated by the chain rule

item.title: Unsupervised Learning Using Generative Adversarial Training And Clustering
item.description: Table of Contents InfoGAN InfoGAN Unsupervised Learning With Adversarial Training and K-means++ clustering Unsupervised Learning With Adversarial Training and K-means++ clustering InfoGAN InfoGAN Use Latent variables \(c\) maximum the mutual information, but the mutual information is hard to compute, get the variational lower bound is obtained : \begin{equation} L_I(G,Q)=\mathbb{E}_{\mathbf{c}\sim P(\mathbf{c}),\mathbf{z}\sim P_{noise}(\mathbf{z})}[\log Q(\mathbf{c}|G(\mathbf{c},\mathbf{z}))]+H(\mathbf{c}) \end{equation} The InfoGAN objective is a regularized version of the original GAN objective:

item.title: Variational Approaches for Auto-Encoding Generative Adversarial Networks
item.description: Table of Contents Overcoming Intractability in Generative Models Latent Variable Models The Density Ratio Trick Variational Inference Synthetic Likelihoods Implicit Variational Distributions Likelihood Choice Hybrid Loss Function Algorithm Algorithm Architectures Overcoming Intractability in Generative Models Latent Variable Models Latent variable models describe a stochastic process by which modeled data is assumed to be generated (and thereby a process by which synthetic data can be simulated from the model distribution).

item.title: Video Tracking Using Learned Hierarchical Features
item.description: Tracking System Overview TODO ASLSA(adaptive structural local sparse appearance model) Tracking System Overview Briefly Introduction of the Tracking System Suppose we have an observation set of target \(x_{1:t}={x_1,\cdots,x_t}\) , a corresponding feature representation set \(z_{1:t}={z_1,\cdots,z_t}\), the target state \(y_t\) can be calculated by: \begin{equation} \label{eq:1} y_t=arg\max_{y_t^i} p(y_t^i|z_{1:t}) \end{equation} where \(y_t^i\) denotes the \(i^{th}\) sample in the \(t^{th}\) frame. Tracking System Overview Briefly Introduction of the Tracking System The posterior probability \(p(y_t|z_{1:t})\) can be inferred by the Bayes’ theorem as follows:

item.title: Word Embedding
item.description: Table of Contents Word Embedding Basic Idea Basic Iead Example Result N-Gram Language Modeling The Continuous Bag-of-Words model (CBOW) Sequence Models and Long-Short Term Memory Networks Bi-LSTM Conditional Random Field Discussion Word Embedding Basic Idea If each attribute is a dimension, then we might give each word a vector, like this: \[ q_\text{mathematician} = \left[ \overbrace{2.3}^\text{can run}, \overbrace{9.4}^\text{likes coffee}, \overbrace{-5.5}^\text{majored in Physics}, \dots \right] \] \[ q_\text{physicist} = \left[ \overbrace{2.

item.title: b-GAN: Unified Framework of Generative Adversarial Networks
f-GAN: Training Generative Neural Samplers using Variational Divergence
item.description: Table of Contents The f-divergence Family The f-divergence Family The f-divergence of \(P\) and \(Q\): What is \(T^{*}(x)\) Variational Divergence Minimization Variational Divergence Minimization Variational Divergence Minimization Variational Divergence Minimization Algorithms for Variational Divergence Minimization (VDM) Single-Step Gradient Method Analysis. Analysis Minimization The f-divergence Family The f-divergence Family f-divergence \begin{eqnarray*} D_f(P||Q) &= & \int_{\mathcal{X}}q(x)f(\frac{p(x)}{q(x)})dx\\ \end{eqnarray*} \(f\) : \(\mathbb{R}_{+}\rightarrow \mathbb{R}\) is a convex, lower-semicontinuous function satisfying \(f(1)=0\) Fenchel conjugate
